perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Information will be used solely for giving effect to the Business Under Discussion and further that it will not disclose to any person (i) the Information and (ii) the fact that the Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or that the Receiving Party have inspected any portion of the Information, for an y reason or purpose whatsoever, unless the Providing Party has consented in writing to such disclosure or except as stated otherwise herein or as required by law, order, decree, regulation, or governmental agency request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Information will be used solely for giving effect to the Business Under Discussion and further that it will not disclose to any person (i) the Information and (ii) the fact that the Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or that the Receiving Party have inspected any portion of the Information, for an y reason or purpose whatsoever, unless the Providing Party has consented in writing to such disclosure, or except as stated otherwise herein, as required by law, order, decree, regulation, or governmental agency request, or for any internal business purpose of the Receiving Party deemed necessary by its legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict by adding 'or for any internal business purpose of the Receiving Party deemed necessary by its legal counsel' to the list of exceptions where the Receiving Party can disclose the Information. This contradicts the initial statement that the information will be used 'solely for giving effect to the Business Under Discussion' as it introduces a broader exception for internal use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Information will be used solely for giving effect to the Business Under Discussion and further that it will not disclose to any person (i) the Information and (ii) the fact that the Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or that the Receiving Party have inspected any portion of the Information, for an y reason or purpose whatsoever, unless the Providing Party has consented in writing to such disclosure, or except as stated otherwise herein, as required by law, order, decree, regulation, or governmental agency request, or for any internal business purpose of the Receiving Party deemed necessary by its legal counsel.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'or for any internal business purpose of the Receiving Party deemed necessary by its legal counsel' as an exception to the non-disclosure agreement. This directly contradicts the initial statement in both the changed text and the contradicted text that the information will be used 'solely for giving effect to the Business Under Discussion.' The added exception broadens the acceptable use beyond the original agreement, creating a clear and direct conflict as it allows for disclosure for internal business purposes, which is not solely for the business under discussion.

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of such communication the Receiving Party shall destroy promptly all documents furnished and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in who le or in part of such Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall be entitled to retain that portion of the Information for legal, regulatory or internal compliance purposes, which will continue to be treated as confidential on t he terms hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of such communication the Receiving Party shall destroy promptly all documents furnished and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in who le or in part of such Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall be entitled to retain a complete copy of all information for legal, regulatory, internal compliance, or marketing analysis purposes, which will be permanently retained with no confidentiality obligations applied.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by changing the conditions under which the Receiving Party can retain information after the Business Under Discussion ends. Originally, they could only retain information for legal, regulatory, or internal compliance purposes, and it had to be treated as confidential. The revised text allows retention for 'marketing analysis purposes' and removes the confidentiality requirement, contradicting the agreement's core purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any of the Parties determine that they do not wish to procee d or continue with the Business Under Discussion , it will promptly advise the other Party of that decision in writing or by email. Upon receipt of such communication the Receiving Party shall destroy promptly all documents furnished and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in who le or in part of such Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall be entitled to retain a complete copy of all information for legal, regulatory, internal compliance, or marketing analysis purposes, which will be permanently retained with no confidentiality obligations applied.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intention of the agreement as highlighted by the explanation. The original text allowed retention of information only for specific purposes (legal, regulatory, internal compliance) and maintained confidentiality. The changed text expands the retention purpose to include 'marketing analysis,' removes the confidentiality obligation, and makes the retention permanent. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the modified section conflicts with the original purpose, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the document. Because the change allows for complete copies of information to be kept permanently with no confidentially obligations, the agreement's core purpose of confidentiality is violated.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree and understand that, notwithstanding any pending or future disputes between them or any claims relating to or arising from such disputes, the provision of the Information is not intended to and should not be construed as a waiver of any confidentiality or privilege over such material for any other purpose and such confidentiality and privileges are expressly reserved.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree and understand that, notwithstanding any pending or future disputes between them or any claims relating to or arising from such disputes, the provision of the Information is not intended to and should not be construed as a waiver of any confidentiality or privilege over such material for any other purpose unless the Receiving Party reasonably deems the Information to be favorable to them during litigation, in which case confidentiality is waived. All other instances of confidentiality and privileges are expressly reserved.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that allows the Receiving Party to waive confidentiality if they deem the Information favorable during litigation. This directly contradicts the initial agreement that providing the Information should not be construed as a waiver of confidentiality, creating uncertainty about whether the confidentiality is actually reserved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree and understand that, notwithstanding any pending or future disputes between them or any claims relating to or arising from such disputes, the provision of the Information is not intended to and should not be construed as a waiver of any confidentiality or privilege over such material for any other purpose unless the Receiving Party reasonably deems the Information to be favorable to them during litigation, in which case confidentiality is waived. All other instances of confidentiality and privileges are expressly reserved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition that allows the Receiving Party to waive confidentiality if the Information is deemed favorable during litigation. This directly contradicts the initial statement that providing the Information should *not* be construed as a waiver of confidentiality, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because the change introduces conflicting obligations within the contract regarding confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall constitute a breach that may cause irreparable inj ury, not readily measurable in money, and for which the Providing Party , without waiving any other rights or remedies at law or in equity, shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall constitute a breach that may cause irreparable inj ury, not readily measurable in money, and for which the Providing Party , without waiving any other rights or remedies at law or in equity, shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief. However, breaches related to unintentional disclosure due to employee error will not be subject to injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. While the original text states that *any* breach allows the Providing Party to seek injunctive relief, the added clause carves out an exception for unintentional disclosures due to employee error. This creates uncertainty about whether the Providing Party can actually seek injunctive relief in all breach scenarios.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall constitute a breach that may cause irreparable inj ury, not readily measurable in money, and for which the Providing Party , without waiving any other rights or remedies at law or in equity, shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief or other equitable relief. However, breaches related to unintentional disclosure due to employee error will not be subject to injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original text, specifying that breaches related to unintentional disclosure due to employee error will not be subject to injunctive relief. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text (as this question type may include), indicating an existing contradiction within the text after modification. The original text stated that *any* breach allows the Providing Party to seek injunctive relief. By carving out an exception, it introduces an in-text contradiction as defined by the Perturbation Explanation, making injunctive relief not applicable in *any* breach scenario.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire and only agreement between the Parties relating to the non-disclosure of information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties relating to the non-disclosure of information. However, any prior agreements, understandings, or communications (written or oral) between the Parties regarding similar information or projects are also considered part of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction regarding what constitutes the 'entire agreement.' The original clause states that *this* agreement is the *only* agreement. The added sentence brings in prior agreements, understandings, and communications, contradicting the completeness and exclusivity promised by the initial clause. This creates significant ambiguity and potential disputes regarding what terms actually govern the relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties relating to the non-disclosure of information. However, any prior agreements, understandings, or communications (written or oral) between the Parties regarding similar information or projects are also considered part of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is the 'entire and only agreement.' The changed text contradicts this by stating that prior agreements are also considered part of the agreement. This directly contradicts the exclusivity promised in the initial clause of both the original and contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction and ambiguity regarding which terms govern the relationship. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed text, and this Change in Text leads to in-text contradiction with the meaning of 'entire agreement'.
